raw_response:
Orders (S1901 Moves)
- F LON -> ENG
- F EDI -> NTH
- A LVP -> YOR

Rationale (brief)
- Secure the English Channel and North Sea immediately — these lanes are our leverage and first line of defense. Holding ENG + NTH reduces the risk of a quick Franco‑German squeeze and keeps options open for support/cooperation.
- A LVP->YOR is cautious landward play: it improves our flexibility in the north without overcommitting to continental entanglements this turn.

Suggested short messages (understated, firm, naval‑focused)

To France (BRE/MAR/PAR):
"I plan to move London->English Channel and Edinburgh->North Sea this turn to secure our sea lanes. I have no immediate wish to contest Brest if you keep to your coasts. A Channel/North Sea clash helps no one; I propose we avoid each other there. If you agree, I’ll not push further east from ENG this season. Thoughts?"

To Germany (KIE/BER/MUN):
"I’m moving a fleet into the North Sea and holding the Channel. That makes a Kie->Hel/Den rush risky for both of us. I prefer a clear northern boundary — you stay east of Denmark/Kiel, I’ll avoid meddling on your land borders. Open to cooperation to keep any continental hegemon in check."

To everyone (concise broadcast):
"My fleets will hold the Channel and North Sea this spring to keep sea lanes safe. I prefer diplomatic solutions and a balance on the continent; if anyone is looking to test the North Sea, let’s talk first — contested fleets there will be costly and destabilising."

If you want, I can draft tailored replies if France or Germany responds, or adjust moves if you prefer a different tack (more aggressive at Brest, or a conservative double‑hold).
